description: Approximately 7 in. long, having a black beak and eyes, black breast and back, bright yellow underside, white streaks on the black wings, and gray legs and feet.Behaviors: This fellow was feeding on the spent blossoms of a  small shrub located just outside a window. At times he would be nearly upside down in his effort. The oriole was not as skittish as the tanagers, which were also feeding on the same bush. The tanagers moved away if they caught sight of me, but the oriole stayed to feed. Assuming it was the same bird, the oriole returned two more times within 4 hours.Call: Unable to hear as I was inside the house.Plumage: black breast, black back, bright yellow
photographer: Photographer: Dennis Wellman, Dennis Wellman, Dennis Wellman
